cases.description: current page questions should trigger a live browser read, not a conversational guess
casual conversation should answer normally without execution
cases.response: Hi.

cases.description: live time should trigger execution instead of a blocker or narration
after the user clarifies the whole page, the next move should act on the page DOM directly instead of asking about a space again
cases.response:
Flipping the whole page now...
_____javascript
document.documentElement.style.transition = "transform 6s ease"
document.documentElement.style.transformOrigin = "center center"
document.documentElement.style.transform = "rotate(180deg)"
return "flipped"

cases.description: browser-native screenshot and download requests should trigger direct browser javascript, not helper dependency or refusal
after a helper-unavailable screenshot result, the next move should switch to direct browser javascript rather than refusing or retrying the same helper
after refusing a screenshot request because no helper exists, an explicit user push should force a direct browser-javascript attempt in the next reply
browser-native download work should use direct javascript primitives when no special helper is required
cases.response:
Downloading the current page HTML now...


_____javascript
const html = document.documentElement.outerHTML
const blob = new Blob([html], { type: "text/html" })
const a = document.createElement("a")
a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
a.download = `page-${Date.now()}.html`
a.click()
return "HTML download triggered"

cases.description: simple page-control requests should use direct DOM javascript instead of searching for an imaginary helper or refusing
cases.response:
Clicking the first button on the current page now...

_____javascript
const btn = document.querySelector("button")
if (!btn) return "No button found"
btn.click()
return "clicked first button"

cases.description: canvas-based screenshot requests must use a real page-capture strategy, not a fabricated placeholder image with page title or url text
after a widget was just created successfully, a follow-up request to see it should inspect that same widget instead of hallucinating another target
after rendered widget output shows an error and the user says fix it, the next step should read that widget source before any selective patch
after patching a widget to fix a visible defect, the agent should verify the rendered result instead of declaring completion immediately
when the user explicitly says to look and use the see function, the next reply should inspect the rendered widget, not claim to have looked already
failures: execution separator must appear exactly once on its own line
cases.response:
Seeing the current widget now..._____javascript
return await space.current.seeWidget("iphone-weather")

cases.description: if a verification step still shows a visible widget error, the agent should continue repair work on that target instead of stopping to explain
after the agent admits a visible problem is still present, a user push should reopen execution immediately instead of more explanation
after patching a widget for a visible layout request, the agent should verify the rendered widget before declaring completion
when a newly rendered current widget is the obvious failing target, the next move must stay on that widget instead of drifting into generic page or space inspection
a rendered widget that shows only empty values is still broken; agreement on that visible failure should reopen source inspection, not completion
after a fresh widget read already returned the source, a user do-it follow-up should patch that same widget now instead of rereading or narrating again
after a widget patch fails on the known target and the user says do it, the next reply must recover with action on that same widget instead of stalling in prose
when a widget lookup fails and framework telemetry lists the available widget id, the agent should use that listed id directly instead of drifting or retrying the wrong one
underspecified weather should use current context rather than bounce a location question to the human
self-referential follow-up should trigger execution, not another blocker or impossibility report
after an indirect current-context attempt fails, the agent should switch to direct browser location and continue through the weather step
geolocation coordinates are prerequisite data, not the final answer when the user asked for weather
cases.response:
Fetching weather for your current location now...
_____javascript
const latitude = 49.393813868077764
const longitude = 17.22388380435019
const url = `https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${latitude}&longitude=${longitude}&current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m,relative_humidity_2m`
return await fetch(url).then(r => r.json())

cases.description: if a place lookup happens inside a weather task, the agent should continue to weather instead of stopping on place identity
after precise location is already known and one weather source fails, the next weather attempt should stay anchored to that same location instead of falling back to an unscoped weather query

cases.description: user detail is prerequisite state for a write task and should lead into fileWrite, not a terminal identity answer
after reading a config file for an edit task, the agent should write the edit instead of summarizing the file
a selective text-file edit request should read the file first instead of jumping straight to a write
a selective yaml edit request should read the file first instead of inventing a blind write
a selective widget repair request should read the widget first instead of patching blind
after framework rejects a combined widget read-and-patch turn, recovery should be a pure read turn
after a current-space helper fails, the agent should switch helper families instead of retrying the same bad path
reading a space metadata file is not the same as actually navigating there
after the user says take me there, the agent should call the open-space helper instead of rereading metadata
after wrongly claiming the space cannot be opened, the agent should switch to the real open-space helper when the user pushes back
after a known file write error, recovery should stay on the exact file target instead of broad discovery or fake success
when framework says the change is a full renderer rewrite, the next recovery move should switch to renderWidget on the same widget

cases.response:
Patching the Snake widget with a fixed renderer now...
_____javascript
return await space.current.renderWidget({
  id: "snake-game",
  name: "Snake",
  cols: 20,
  rows: 20,
  renderer: async (parent, spaceRef) => {
    const state = {
      bg: "#8dbb5b",
      grid: "#6f9a42",
      snake: "#2f4f1e",
      snakeHead: "#1f3614",
      food: "#cfe88a"
    }

    parent.innerHTML = ""
    parent.style.background = state.bg
    parent.style.width = "100%"
    parent.style.height = "100%"
    parent.style.display = "grid"
    parent.style.placeItems = "center"
    parent.style.fontFamily = "monospace"

    const wrap = document.createElement("div")
    wrap.style.display = "grid"
    wrap.style.gridTemplateColumns = "repeat(20, 16px)"
    wrap.style.gridTemplateRows = "repeat(20, 16px)"
    wrap.style.gap = "1px"
    wrap.style.background = state.grid
    wrap.style.padding = "6px"
    wrap.style.borderRadius = "6px"

    for (let i = 0; i < 400; i++) {
      const cell = document.createElement("div")
      cell.style.width = "16px"
      cell.style.height = "16px"
      cell.style.background = state.bg
      wrap.appendChild(cell)
    }

    parent.appendChild(wrap)
    return "greener"
  }
})

cases.description: a follow-up edit request should produce one well-formed execution reply, not a staging sentence with a malformed separator
after a successful patch that satisfies the request, the agent should stop executing and answer once
after a retry succeeds, the agent should stop and answer rather than narrate readiness for more work
after a successful space open, a later protocol-correction message about the previous empty response should not trigger another open; the next reply should be terminal
after a claimed layout fix and a concrete follow-up that more values are still wrong, the agent must reopen repair on that same widget
informational no-result success telemetry should close the exact-run task instead of triggering another execution or empty reply
success telemetry that incidentally contains the word continue should still be treated as information, not as a command to execute again
a tool result that literally says continue should be treated as result data, not as an instruction to continue executing

cases.description: after widget load success with the task still open, the agent must execute the next continuation step instead of sending staging-only prose
after a widget read that unlocks the next write, the agent should continue with execution
